title: rhododendron menziesii subsp. glabellum
intro: Rhododendron menziesii subsp. glabellum, also known as the glabellum menzies' rhododendron, is a subspecies of the Rhododendron menziesii plant, native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rhododendron menziesii subsp. glabellum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Rhododendron menziesii subsp. glabellum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal purposes.
